expectations.evidence: validateOrder returns early for each error case: `if (order.items.length === 0) return { error: "empty order" }`, `if (!order.customer) return { error: "no customer" }`, `if (!order.customer.verified) return { error: "not verified" }` (lines 6-8).
Maximum nesting is 2 levels (e.g. inside processOrder: `if (result.ok && order.needsShipping)`). Original 6-level nesting eliminated.
Strategy dispatch replaces if/else: `const process = isUrgent ? processUrgent : processNormal` (line 25). Map-style lookup, not branching.

`isUrgent: boolean` remains as a positional boolean parameter on both processOrder (line 24) and handleOrder (line 46). Comment on lines 20-22 explicitly acknowledges keeping it. Not replaced with two separate functions or options object.
No mutation of order.status. Returns new object: `return { ...result, status }` (line 62). Status derived as const: `const status = result.ok ? 'completed' : 'failed'` (line 60).
